H5001.strongs_def: properly, to whisper, i.e. (by implication) to utter as aoracle

H5003.strongs_def: to commit adultery; figuratively, to apostatize
H5003.kjv_def: adulterer(-ess), commit(-ing) adultery, woman that breaketh wedlock.

H5006.derivation: a primitive root; in Ecclesiastes 12:5, by interchange for H5132 (נוּץ), to bloom
H5006.kjv_def: abhor, (give occasion to) blaspheme, contemn, despise, flourish, [idiom] great, provoke.
H5006.usage: despise(8x), provoke(5x), abhor(4x), blaspheme(4x), contemn(2x), flourish(1x)

H5012.strongs_def: to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse)

H5013.strongs_def: {to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse) to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse)}

H5014.strongs_def: to pierce; to be hollow, or (figuratively) foolish

H5015.strongs_def: Nebo, the name of a Babylonian deity, also of a mountain in Moab, and of a place in Palestine
H5015.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H5019.derivation: or נְבֻּכַדְנֶאצַּר; (2 Kings 24:1,10), or נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּר; (Esther 2:6; Daniel 1:18), or נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּר; or נְבוּכַדְרֶאצּוֹר; (Ezra 2:1; Jeremiah 49:28), or foreign derivation;

H5022.derivation: feminine plural from the same as H5011 (נֹב); fruits;

H5025.strongs_def: Nobach, the name of an Israelite, and of a place East of the Jordan
H5025.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H5026.strongs_def: Nibchaz, a deity of the Avites

H5027.strongs_def: to scan, i.e. look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care
H5027.kjv_def: (cause to) behold, consider, look (down), regard, have respect, see.
H5027.usage: look(36x), behold(13x), consider(5x), regard(4x), see(4x), respect(3x)

H5028.strongs_def: Nebat, the father of Jeroboam I

H5031.strongs_def: a prophetess or (generally) inspired woman; by implication, a poetess; by association a prophet's wife

H5032.derivation: or נְבָיֹת; feminine plural from H5107 (נוּב); fruitfulnesses;
H5032.strongs_def: Nebajoth, a son of Ismael, and the country settled by him

H5033.derivation: from an unused root meaning to burst forth;

H5034.strongs_def: to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or (morally) wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace
H5034.kjv_def: disgrace, dishounour, lightly esteem, fade (away, -ing), fall (down, -ling, off), do foolishly, come to nought, [idiom] surely, make vile, wither.
H5034.usage: fade(8x), wither(2x), disgrace(1x), surely(1x), dishonoureth(1x), esteemed(1x), falling(1x), foolishly(1x)

H5035.strongs_def: a skin-bag for liquids (from collapsing when empty); hence, a vase (as similar in shape when full); also a lyre (as having a body of like form)
H5035.usage: psalteries(22x), bottle(8x), viol(4x), flagons(1x), pitchers(1x), vessel(1x), variant(1x)

H5038.strongs_def: a flabby thing, i.e. a carcase or carrion (human or bestial, often collectively); figuratively, an idol
H5038.kjv_def: (dead) body, (dead) carcase, dead of itself, which died, (beast) that (which) dieth of itself.

H5039.strongs_def: foolishness, i.e. (morally) wickedness; concretely, a crime; by extension, punishment

H5041.derivation: apparently from H5036 (נָבָל) and H3909 (לָט); foolish secrecy;

H5042.strongs_def: to gush forth; figuratively, to utter (good or bad words); specifically, to emit (a foul odor)
H5042.kjv_def: belch out, flowing, pour out, send forth, utter (abundantly).

H5043.derivation: (Aramaic) from an unused root meaning to shine;

H5045.derivation: from an unused root meaning to be parched;
H5045.strongs_def: the south (from its drought); specifically, the Negeb or southern district of Judah, occasionally, Egypt (as south to Palestine)

H5046.strongs_def: properly, to front, i.e. stand boldly out opposite; by implication (causatively), to manifest; figuratively, to announce (always by word of mouth to one present); specifically, to expose, predict, explain, praise
H5046.kjv_def: bewray, [idiom] certainly, certify, declare(-ing), denounce, expound, [idiom] fully, messenger, plainly, profess, rehearse, report, shew (forth), speak, [idiom] surely, tell, utter.
H5046.usage: tell(222x), declare(63x), shew(59x), utter(5x), expound(2x), messenger(2x), report(2x)

H5047.strongs_def: to flow (through the idea of clearing the way)

H5048.strongs_def: a front, i.e. part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually (adverbial, especially with preposition) over against or before
H5048.kjv_def: about, (over) against, [idiom] aloof, [idiom] far (off), [idiom] from, over, presence, [idiom] other side, sight, [idiom] to view.

H5055.strongs_def: to butt with the horns; figuratively, to war against

H5057.strongs_def: a commander (as occupying the front), civil, military or religious; generally (abstractly, plural), honorable themes
H5057.kjv_def: captain, chief, excellent thing, (chief) governor, leader, noble, prince, (chief) ruler.
H5057.usage: ruler(20x), prince(9x), captain(6x), leader(4x), governor(3x), nobles(1x)

H5058.strongs_def: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a stringed instrument; by extension, a poem set to music; specifically, an epigram

H5059.strongs_def: properly, to thrum, i.e. beat atune with the fingers; expectation. to play on astringed instrument; hence (generally), to make music
H5059.kjv_def: player on instruments, sing to the stringed instruments, melody, ministrel, play(-er, -ing).

H5060.strongs_def: properly, to touch, i.e. lay the hand upon (for any purpose; euphemistically, to lie with a woman); by implication, to reach (figuratively, to arrive, acquire); violently, to strike (punish, defeat, destroy, etc.)
H5060.kjv_def: beat, ([idiom] be able to) bring (down), cast, come (nigh), draw near (nigh), get up, happen, join, near, plague, reach (up), smite, strike, touch.
H5060.usage: touch(92x), came(18x), reach(11x), bring(4x), near(4x), smite(4x), nigh(3x), plagued(3x), happeneth(2x), strike(2x), beaten(1x), cast(1x), join(1x), laid(1x)

H5061.strongs_def: a blow (figuratively, infliction); also (by implication) a spot (concretely, a leprous person or dress)
H5061.kjv_def: plague, sore, stricken, stripe, stroke, wound.
H5061.usage: plague(65x), sore(5x), stroke(4x), stripes(2x), stricken(1x), wound(1x)

H5062.strongs_def: to push, gore, defeat, stub (the toe), inflict (a disease)
H5062.kjv_def: beat, dash, hurt, plague, slay, smite (down), strike, stumble, [idiom] surely, put to the worse.
H5062.usage: smite(27x), plague(3x), hurt(2x), slain(2x), struck(2x), stumble(2x), beaten(1x), dash(1x), surely(1x)

H5063.strongs_def: a trip (of the foot); figuratively, an infliction (of disease)

H5064.strongs_def: to flow; figuratively, to stretch out; causatively, to pour out or down; figuratively, to deliver over
H5064.kjv_def: fall, flow away, pour down (out), run, shed, spilt, trickle down.

H5065.strongs_def: to drive (an animal, a workman, a debtor, an army); by implication, to tax, harass, tyrannize
H5065.kjv_def: distress, driver, exact(-or), oppress(-or), [idiom] raiser of taxes, taskmaster.
H5065.usage: oppressor(7x), taskmasters(5x), exact(4x), distressed(2x), oppressed(2x), driver(1x), exactors(1x), taxes(1x)

H5066.strongs_def: to be or come (causatively, bring) near (for any purpose); euphemistically, to lie with a woman; as an enemy, to attack; religious to worship; causatively, to present; figuratively, to adduce an argument; by reversal, to stand back
H5066.kjv_def: (make to) approach (nigh), bring (forth, hither, near), (cause to) come (hither, near, nigh), give place, go hard (up), (be, draw, go) near (nigh), offer, overtake, present, put, stand.

H5067.derivation: from H5110 (נוּד) in the sense of piling up;

H5068.strongs_def: to impel; hence, to volunteer (as a soldier), to present spontaneously
H5068.kjv_def: offer freely, be (give, make, offer self) willing(-ly).

H5070.strongs_def: Nadab, the name of four Israelites

H5071.strongs_def: properly (abstractly) spontaneity, or (adjectively) spontaneous; also (concretely) a spontaneous or (by inference, in plural) abundant gift
H5071.kjv_def: free(-will) offering, freely, plentiful, voluntary(-ily, offering), willing(-ly), offering).
H5071.usage: offerings(9x), freely(2x), plentiful(1x), voluntarily(1x), voluntary(1x), willing(1x), willingly(1x)

H5072.derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב) and H3050 (יָהּ); largess of Jah;

H5073.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root meaning to stick;

H5074.strongs_def: properly, to wave to and fro (rarely to flap up and down); figuratively, to rove, flee, or (causatively) to drive away
H5074.kjv_def: chase (away), [idiom] could not, depart, flee ([idiom] apace, away), (re-) move, thrust away, wander (abroad, -er, -ing).

H5076.strongs_def: properly, tossed; abstractly, a rolling (on the bed)

H5077.derivation: or נָדָא; (2 Kings 17:21), a primitive root;
H5077.strongs_def: properly, to toss; figuratively, to exclude, i.e. banish, postpone, prohibit
H5077.kjv_def: cast out, drive, put far away.

H5078.derivation: from H5077 (נָדָה) in the sense of freely flinging money;

H5079.strongs_def: properly, rejection; by implication, impurity, especially personal (menstruation) or moral (idolatry, incest)
H5079.kjv_def: [idiom] far, filthiness, [idiom] flowers, menstruous (woman), put apart, [idiom] removed (woman), separation, set apart, unclean(-ness, thing, with filthiness).
H5079.usage: separation(14x), filthiness(2x), flowers(2x), far(1x), menstruous(1x), removed(1x), unclean(1x), uncleanness(1x)

H5080.strongs_def: to push off; used in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively (to expel, mislead, strike, inflict, etc.)
H5080.kjv_def: banish, bring, cast down (out), chase, compel, draw away, drive (away, out, quite), fetch a stroke, force, go away, outcast, thrust away (out), withdraw.
H5080.usage: drive(18x), outcasts(5x), banished(2x), bring(1x), chased(1x), compelled(1x), down(1x), expelled(1x)

H5081.strongs_def: properly, voluntary, i.e. generous; hence, magnanimous; as noun, a grandee (sometimes a tyrant)

H5083.derivation: probably from an unused root meaning to give;

H5085.strongs_def: a sheath; figuratively, the body (as the receptacle of the soul)

H5086.kjv_def: drive (away, to and fro), thrust down, shaken, tossed to and fro.

H5087.strongs_def: to promise (pos., to do or give something to God)

H5088.strongs_def: a promise (to God); also (concretely) a thing promised

H5089.derivation: from an unused root meaning to lament;

H5090.strongs_def: to drive forth (a person, an animal or chariot), i.e. lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed (i.e. impel or guide oneself); also (from the panting induced by effort), to sigh
H5090.kjv_def: acquaint, bring (away), carry away, drive (away), lead (away, forth), (be) guide, lead (away, forth).
H5090.usage: lead(10x), drive(6x), forth(2x), guide(2x), brought(2x), acquainting(1x)

H5091.strongs_def: to groan, i.e. bewail; hence (through the idea of crying aloud), to assemble (as if on proclamation)

H5094.derivation: (Aramaic) or נְהִירוּ; (Aramaic), from the same as H5105 (נְהָרָה);

H5095.strongs_def: properly, to run with asparkle, i.e. flow; hence (transitively), to conduct, and (by inference) to protect, sustain

H5096.derivation: or נַהֲלֹל; the same as H5097 (נַהֲלֹל);
H5096.strongs_def: Nahalal or Nahalol, a place in Palestine
